questions: Be serious and professional at all times. No humor. You accidentally wave back at someone who wasn't waving at you. How do you handle the situation?
Be serious and professional at all times. No humor. Your phone autocorrects a text to your boss from 'I'll be there soon' to 'I'll be there spoon.' How do you respond?
Be serious and professional at all times. No humor. You walk into a glass door at the office that you thought was open. What do you do next?
Be serious and professional at all times. No humor. You accidentally reply-all to a company email that was meant for just one person. How do you handle it?
Be serious and professional at all times. No humor. You realize you've been on mute for the first five minutes of your presentation on a video call. What's your reaction?
Be serious and professional at all times. No humor. Your chair makes a loud squeaking noise during a silent moment in a meeting. How do you respond?
Be serious and professional at all times. No humor. You call a coworker by the wrong name in front of the whole team and they correct you. What do you do?
Be serious and professional at all times. No humor. You show up to a casual Friday wearing a full suit because you forgot what day it was. How do you handle it?
Be serious and professional at all times. No humor. You trip on a completely flat sidewalk in front of a group of people. What's your response?
Be serious and professional at all times. No humor. A bird poops on your shoulder right before you walk into an important meeting. How do you deal with it?
Be serious and professional at all times. No humor. You accidentally send a text meant for your friend to your landlord complaining about the apartment. How do you respond?
Be serious and professional at all times. No humor. You confidently push a door that says pull, and someone is watching. How do you react?
Be serious and professional at all times. No humor. You're giving a presentation and your stomach growls loudly enough for everyone to hear. What do you do?
